hero.title: 4 Days in Mumbai.
hero.titleAccent: The City of Dreams and the Magic of Bollywood.
hero.intro: Mumbai never stops. India's biggest, brashest, most energetic city, home to Bollywood, colonial grandeur, street food legends and the gateway to the Arabian Sea. Four days in the city of dreams.
hero.heroImageAlt: Mumbai Gateway of India and skyline

overview.heading: 4 Days Mumbai + Bollywood: The Full Picture
body: Mumbai is India turned up to maximum. The financial and entertainment capital, it blends Victorian Gothic and Art Deco architecture, the buzz of Bollywood, some of the country's best street food, and the relentless energy of a city of over twenty million people. It is exhilarating.

This 4 day Mumbai package from Malaysia is private throughout, with your own vehicle and driver guide. You see the Gateway of India, the grand colonial precinct, the Dhobi Ghat open-air laundry, Marine Drive at sunset, and get a taste of Bollywood with a studio visit. The city's contrasts make it endlessly fascinating.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Gateway of India & Colonial Mumbai
items.body: The iconic Gateway of India arch by the harbour, the grand Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus, and the elegant colonial and Art Deco buildings that line the city centre.

items.title: Bollywood & the City of Dreams
items.body: A glimpse behind the scenes of the world's most prolific film industry, with a Bollywood studio visit and the stories of the stars who made Mumbai the city of dreams.

items.title: Marine Drive, Street Food & Markets
items.body: Sunset on the Queen's Necklace at Marine Drive, the legendary street food of Mohammed Ali Road and Chowpatty, and the bustling markets of a city that never sleeps.

days.title: Arrive Mumbai
days.description: Met at Mumbai airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening at Marine Drive.

days.title: Mumbai City Tour
days.description: A full day exploring colonial Mumbai and its landmarks.

days.title: Bollywood & Elephanta
days.description: A Bollywood studio visit and optional ferry to the Elephanta caves.

days.title: Depart Mumbai
days.description: Checkout and transfer to the airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: Mumbai, Navigated for You
whyBook.body: Mumbai's scale and traffic can overwhelm, but a private vehicle and a local guide turn it into an exhilarating, easy city break. We know the timings, the food and the Bollywood angles. Dhesu has run India city tours for over 35 years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Mumbai & Bollywood Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
Mumbai is one of the most exciting city breaks in India, and a private tour is the best way to experience it given the scale and energy. This 4 day package covers the colonial landmarks, the Bollywood magic and the street food legends.

Your own vehicle and driver guide handle the famous Mumbai traffic, so you cover the Gateway of India, Marine Drive and the studios with ease.

Mumbai also pairs beautifully with Goa for a city-and-beach combination, covered in our 5 day package.

sections.heading: Bollywood in Mumbai
sections.body:
Mumbai is the home of Bollywood, the world's most prolific film industry. A studio visit offers a glimpse behind the scenes of the song-and-dance spectacles that captivate audiences across Asia.

The city is dotted with film history, from star homes in Bandra to iconic shooting locations. For film fans, it is a highlight of any Mumbai trip.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (BOM). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Mumbai?
items.answer: October to February offers the most pleasant weather, dry and relatively cool. The monsoon (June to September) brings heavy rain and dramatic seas but can disrupt sightseeing. March to May is hot and humid.

items.question: Is the Bollywood studio visit guaranteed?
items.answer: We arrange a Bollywood studio or city film tour as part of the package. Access to active shoots depends on schedules, but the tour offers a genuine behind-the-scenes glimpse of the industry. We confirm the specific experience when you book.

items.question: Can I combine Mumbai with Goa?
items.answer: Yes. Mumbai and Goa make a great city-and-beach pairing. Our 5 day Mumbai plus Goa package combines the energy of the city with the beaches and Portuguese heritage of Goa.

cta.heading: Ready for the city of dreams?
